evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Keeping data quality high and holding the line on our data contracts
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Data contracts, testing standards, quality, and documentation recur across multiple responsibility bullets with no velocity/shipping-speed phrases present, producing a rigour classification.
evidence.domain_risk: give the team visibility on how their features perform and a clear picture of what customers do with what they build
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: An e-commerce/fashion marketplace context where errors affect product and commercial decisions but carry no regulatory or financial-reporting consequence, so moderate applies.
evidence.collaboration_width: customer experience tribe; engineers; business stakeholders
evidence.data_team_maturity: We record nearly 100 million lines of data every day and model it across roughly 600 dbt models
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A central Data Platform team operating ~600 dbt models at ~100M rows/day, embedded product squads, and a named data platform engineer signal an established, specialised data function at scale.
evidence.jd_authorship: We record nearly 100 million lines of data every day and model it across roughly 600 dbt models that power the answers to the product and commercial questions we face.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific scale numbers, a named tech stack (Snowflake, dbt, Omni, Monte Carlo, Terraform, Snowpipe), and concrete project examples (migrating Looker to Omni) let the reader clearly understand what the role does day to day.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: partner closely with a product development squad in our customer experience tribe ... power the answers to the product and commercial questions we face
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role explicitly spans both product (embedded partnership with a product squad, feature performance) and commercial/central-platform work with roughly equal weight, producing a mixed orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: You will help us grow and improve that foundation so the business can go further with it.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role combines central platform ownership ("helping deliver our wider data strategy") with embedded execution support for a product squad ("partnering with your squad... to model the data behind their features"), a genuine mixed autonomy signal.
evidence.ai_role: Building the models and semantic layer that let stakeholders and AI tools get trustworthy answers on their own
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to build the semantic layer and data foundations that AI tools consume to answer questions, making the AI a downstream consumer of the candidate's infrastructure — an ai_enabler signal that also coexists with an ai_user requirement ("comfortable using AI tools in your own workflow"), and per the tie-breaker both-present resolves to ai_enabler.
evidence.testing_framing: Working with engineers and our data platform engineer to raise data quality and testing standards
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing standards and data contracts are named as something the candidate actively raises and holds the line on, an ownership framing rather than a listed tool.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Keeping data quality high and holding the line on our data contracts
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality and contracts recur as an operational concern across several bullets, but the JD's dominant framing is growth, AI transformation, and craft development rather than compliance or stakeholder-trust risk, placing this at moderate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: You will help us grow and improve that foundation so the business can go further with it.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The dominant verb signal across the infrastructure tasks is improving, optimising, and migrating existing systems (warehouse, BI stack, testing standards) rather than building new ones from scratch, so fix_scale applies.
evidence.work_arrangement: We all come into the office on Tuesdays and Thursdays, with the option to work remotely or come into the office on the other days.
evidence.loss_aversion: Keeping data quality high and holding the line on our data contracts — a recurring but secondary concern beneath the JD's dominant growth/AI-transformation narrative.
